item.title: Greco EGB-850 Short Scale Les Paul Bass Black MIJ Fujigen Japan Vintage 1985 — $1,257.65
item.description:
$1,257.65 · Good · Lucky cat Guitar market(JAPAN)

This is a Greco EGB-850 Les Paul Bass made in Japan. Based on the serial number, I believe it was made in 1985. However, there is also a possibility that it may be from 1995.

This is a popular Fujigen-made model. It has been carefully maintained by a professional repair technician, so it is ready to play as soon as it arrives. If you have been looking for one, please do not miss this opportunity!

Body: Mahogany with Maple Top
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Rosewood

Weight: Approx. 4.6 kg

There are several noticeable scratches, but they have been touched up. As mentioned above, it has been professionally serviced, including fret leveling, full setup, and cleaning. The electronics are also working properly with no issues.

Included accessory: Non-original gig bag.
Please note that one side of the shoulder strap is damaged, so please consider it mainly for shipping and storage purposes.



Please note that this is a used item.
There are no issues with its current functionality, but please understand that you may need to make further adjustments after purchase to suit your preferences.
Be sure to review the photos and description carefully before placing a bid.
If there’s anything you’d like to confirm, please don’t hesitate to contact us.


Important Notice

Payments are accepted via PayPal only.

Please ensure the shipping address in your PayPal account exactly matches the one registered on Reverb.

We will ship to the Reverb-registered address even if you use Guest Checkout or if the PayPal address does not match. However, PayPal Buyer/Seller Protection may not apply in such cases.

PayPal transaction fees are non-refundable on any cancellation.


Customs and Import Regulations

We do not undervalue items or mark them as “gifts,” as this is prohibited by regulations in Japan, the United States, and other countries.

Import duties, taxes, and any other charges are the buyer’s responsibility. Please check with your local customs office in advance.

In rare cases, especially for high-value items, U.S. Customs or the carrier may request the buyer’s Tax Identification Number (such as an SSN or CBP Form 5106) for clearance. This is a standard procedure, and failure to provide it may result in delays or the return of the package.


Thank you for your understanding!


Management Number: E-2633

item.title: Music and more MAM MB33 RETRO 1996 — $595.62
item.description:
$595.62 · Very Good · Boutique Tek


For Sale: MAM MB33 — A True ’90s Acid Machine

À vendre : MAM MB33 — une véritable machine à acid des années 90.

A German-made analog clone inspired by the legendary Roland TB-303, the MB33 delivers powerful acid basslines, aggressive resonance, and a unique vintage character. It’s perfect for all kinds of music production.

It is in very good condition considering its age. I have the original box and the original power supply (see photos).

Features:

  • ✅ 100% analog sound
  • ✅ Made in Germany
  • ✅ MIDI integrated
  • ✅ Very easy and efficient to program
  • ✅ Works perfectly
  • ✅ Ultra-compact

It comes with its original 12V AC power supply.

Feel free to make an offer! 🙂



Clone analogique fabriqué en Allemagne, inspiré de la légendaire Roland TB-303, le MB33 produit des lignes de basse acid puissantes, une résonance agressive et un caractère vintage unique. Il convient à tous les styles de production musicale.


Il est en très bon état compte tenu de son âge , je possède la boite et l’adaptateur secteur d’origine (voir les photos).

item.title: 【used】dragonfly / IL CUORE 5st 4.345kg #3059S【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,869
item.description:
$2,869 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Dragonfly is a Japanese guitar and bass brand with a long history.

They manufacture high-quality products using knowledge and technology cultivated over many years.

It is favored by many musicians, and it is well known that Teyan Tetsuya of Fear, and Loathing in Las Vegas uses a Dragonfly bass. (Titles omitted)

Dragonfly has several models, but this is the IL CUORE, a high-end line made using carefully selected woods.

Since a spec sheet is not included, the wood type is judged from the appearance.

The body top is rosewood, and the body back is ash.

The neck is a 5-piece laminate type mainly of maple combined with walnut-type woods.

This method of laminating multiple types of wood to create the neck minimizes left-right and individual differences, resulting in higher stability.

The pickups are original.

The preamp is a Bartolini TCT.

The bridge and tuning machines are made by GOTOH.

With its double-cutaway body design, this bass offers a familiar balance, even for those who primarily play Fender-style basses.

The sound is sharp and clear, like a Jazz Bass.

At the same time, it possesses a refined nuance, giving it a modern feel.

Based on a sound with universal appeal, this model's major strengths lie in its wide tonal range thanks to its 24 frets and its flexible preamp.

This is a high-end model that combines meticulous craftsmanship with highly practical sound shaping capabilities.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some scratches and dents overall. There are dents of about 10.0mm on the back of the neck at the 5th and 8th frets.

The electronics are in good condition.

The volume pot appears to have been replaced, causing the knob to be slightly crooked. Currently, there are no functional problems.
Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Treble, Bass, Master Tone, Middle (in cavity)
Nut Width: 49.2mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 35 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

During setup, we use our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

They are delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Semi-hard case

Text: Mizune Ishikawa

Inspection 담당: Hirayama

Product Photos: Higashie

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

◯GIB and TONIQ Warranty Information

●Lifetime Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

item.title: Atelier Z M265 – Natural – 5-String Bass – 4.36kg – Made in Japan — $2,558.69
item.description:
$2,558.69 · Brand New · RockSo Music

Hand-built in Japan, the Atelier Z M265 is a performance-focused 5-string bass designed for players who need extended range with clarity, balance, and control.

This bass is in stock at RockSo Music and ready to play — avoiding the typical 8–12 month wait for a new Atelier Z order.

The M265 delivers a powerful and articulate response across all five strings, with a focused low B and consistent definition throughout the register. It is well suited for players who want extended range without sacrificing note clarity or playing feel.

The combination of an Ash body and Maple 1-piece neck provides fast attack, strong articulation, and clear note separation.

Equipped with the Bartolini XTCT + Spectrum Boost preamp, the M265 offers flexible and musical tone shaping via a 3-band EQ. The XTCT circuit — a variant of the well-known TCT platform associated with Marcus Miller — provides excellent headroom and clarity while preserving the instrument’s natural character.

Specifications:

Year: 2026
34" scale
5-string configuration
Ash body
Maple 1-piece neck (21 frets)
Atelier Z JBZ-5 pickups
Bartolini XTCT + Spectrum Boost active preamp (3-band EQ)
Gotoh GB528 tuners
Atelier Z BB518 bridge (18mm spacing)
Weight: 4.36kg

Condition:
Brand new. Includes original Atelier Z deluxe gig bag.

Ships fast worldwide.
Import duties and local taxes may apply outside the EU.

item.title: Fender Custom Shop '55 Precision Bass 2020 Blonde VOS — $3,967.99
item.description:
$3,967.99 · Excellent · Joel's Vintage Guitars

Now available on consignment, a 2020 Fender Custom Shop '55 Reissue Precision Bass in blonde over ash.

This particular example has been barely played with only a light few marks on the back of the body to note. The neck has a big U-profile right down to the first few frets, and has a 9.5" fingerboard radius with vintage fret wire.

Other specifications include a hand wired '55 single coil pickup, and a NOS nitrocellulose lacquer finish.

Its currently setup with flat-wound strings, includes a custom shop gig bag and some case candy including the COA/spec sheet.

--- Joel's Vintage Guitars is a by appointment vintage & used guitar store located in Sydney, Australia.

All prices are higher to accommodate Reverb fees.

We ship WORLDWIDE! ---

item.title: Aria Pro II RSB LMD-II, MIJ - 1987 — $280.55
item.description:
$280.55 · Fair · Paul's Vintage Japanese Gear Outlet

Good condition Aria Pro II from the mid 80's.

The body has some very light finish scratches and small dings on the back. There is also some general ageing to the hardware/metal parts. There is a finish crack running from the bridge towards the bridge pickup then past the pickup and then past the neck pickup (see pix) but it looks to be stable and not an issue. The neck is very clean and there is no major damage or dings that I can see. The head stock has some paint chips at the end.

I've replaced the selector switch and input jack.


Electrics: Functioning all ok
Truss rod: Turning and can still tighten.
Frets: About 90% plus left.
Neck: Straight setup now with a slight relief.
Action:    Low E: 2.8mm      G: 2.6mm At the 17th fret (still room at the saddles to come down)
Weight: 4.37 kg        9.63 lb


  • Body: alder
  • Neck: rosewood on maple
  • Grip: thin c
  • Scale: 34" 860mm
  • Nut: 40mm
  • Hardware: original Aria Pro II die cast bridge
  • Tuners: Gotoh
  • Pickups:  original Aria double coil  4AP     /   original Aria 4AJ
  • Controls: 1 x volume knob, 3 way pickup selector toggle, 1 x tone knob

comes with gig bag


Boxed, shipped and tracked via EMS Japan Post.

For shipping to Spain or Australia I'll have to remove the neck due to box length limitations.

All customs duties and fees are the responsibility of the buyer. If you're not sure how things work for your country I'll do my best to answers any questions, to the best of my knowledge, that you may have.

item.title: Yamaha super bass 500s MIJ-1980s — $515.96
item.description:
$515.96 · Very Good · Paul's Vintage Japanese Gear Outlet

Great condition Yamaha Super Bass 500s made in Japan in the early 80s. Check out the video for an idea of the sound. The action is down nice and low at the moment and is playing really nicely up and down the neck.

I refinished the neck with Tru oil and replaced the tuners with a new set of quality Gotoh GB 2 Ns (One of the original tuners was replaced with a Greco tuner and I had trouble finding a matching Yamaha tuner). I've included a pic of the back of the head-stock with the original serial number 002520 before refinishing.

I've also replaced the bridge screws and input jack nut and cleaned up all the hardware and frets.

I've done a basic set-up, action and intonation with .045-.105 strings, but recommend you do your own to suit your playing style and preferred string gauge.

  • Electrics: Original, Functioning all ok.
  • Truss rod: turning smoothly and can still tighten.
  • Frets: about 90% plus left
  • Neck: Straight, set up now with some slight relief.
  • Action: Low E: 2.75 mm  G: 2.50 mm at the 17th fret (still room at the saddles to come down)
  • Weight: 4.38 kg       9.63   lb
  • Hardware: Everything turning smoothly (saddle height screws, Intonation screws, Tuners etc)


Specs

Body: Nato

Neck: Maple 1 piece

Scale: 34"(864mm)

Nut: bone 42mm

Hardware: original Yamaha

Tuners: Gotoh GB 2 N (New)

Pickups: original Yamaha

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone (Passive)


Comes with vintage Yamaha Gig bag.

item.title: Ibanez SR-480/SR-600 MIJ-1994 — $567.56
item.description:
$567.56 · Very Good · Paul's Vintage Japanese Gear Outlet

Great condition mid 1990s SR-480 from the Fujigen factory in Japan and not to be confused with the SR-480 produced by Cortek from the year 2000. The body is an SR-480 with an SR-600 neck and seems to have been assembled as so at the factory. The bass is playing smoothly with a nice action.

It was a little difficult to ID from catalogs but after removing the neck I found some writing in the neck pocket (pics included) which was interesting. As far as I can determine the bass was made as a "sample" order for "Hanaoka" san who would have been part of the sales section at Ibanez. The bass was made using an SR-480 body with true maple top and back and a SR-600 neck. I've only managed to find one other example online of an MIJ SR-480 from the same era.

There's a small hair-line crack in the finish just in front of the bridge pickup running from the top of the body to the top of the pickup. it's probably smaller than a hair and almost impossible to notice. There are also some light scratches on the back of the body Other than that the bass is in great condition overall. The neck has a few very small dings on the back.


I've done a basic set-up, action and intonation with .045-.105 strings, but recommend you do your own to suit your playing style and preferred string gauge.

  • Electrics: Original, Functioning all ok.
  • Truss rod: turning smoothly and can still tighten.
  • Frets: about 90% plus left
  • Neck: Straight, set up now with some slight relief.
  • Action: Low E: 2.75mm  G: 2.50mm at the 17th fret (still room at the saddles to come down)
  • Weight: 3.23 kg        7.12 lb
  • Hardware: Everything turning smoothly (saddle height screws, Intonation screws, Tuners etc)


Specs

Body: Ash or Basswood with Flame Maple top and back.

Neck: Rosewood on Maple 305R, 24 Fret.

Scale: 34"(864mm)

Nut: 38mm

Bridge: EB7

Tuners: Gotoh

Pickups: original Ibanez

Controls: 1 Volume, 1 Pickup Blend knob, 1 Tone (Passive)


Comes with Gig bag.


Boxed, shipped and tracked via EMS Japan Post.

For shipping to Spain or Australia I'll have to remove the neck due to box length limitations.


All customs duties and fees are the responsibility of the buyer. If you're not sure how things work for your country I'll do my best to answers any questions, to the best of my knowledge, that you may have.

item.title: 【used】Fender / American Professional Precision Bass Antique Olive 2019 4.120kg #US19093551【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,022
item.description:
$2,022 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, without a doubt, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including James Jamerson, Bobby Vega, and Michael League. (Titles omitted)

The Precision Bass, the world's first mass-produced fretted solid-body electric bass, instantly captivated bassists worldwide.

Its appeal remains unchanged even more than half a century after its creation, and it continues to be loved by many players.

The body is made of alder, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.

It's equipped with V-Mod Split Single-Coil Precision Bass pickups.

The overall balance is excellent, and you get a refined impression just by holding it and playing a single note.

The sound is based on the traditional thickness and ease of use of the Precision Bass, but with a more modern, warm, and powerful finish.

Even with line recording, it doesn't require excessive correction, and the sound naturally comes across as a bass-like instrument, demonstrating its high level of perfection. This bass combines the classic charm of a Precision Bass with modern playability and stability, striking a high balance between the two.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have some minor scratches, but overall it is clean.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Volume, Tone
Nut Width: 42.0mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2019
Country of Manufacture: USA

During setup, SIT Strings, favored by many professional artists, were used.

They are characterized by stable tuning and a powerful, three-dimensional sound. Please enjoy them as well.

GIB's craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Original hard case

item.title: Hofner VINTAGE Model 500/1 Bass w/Sound Mix late60's — $5,711.04
item.description:
$5,711.04 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

Hofner, which sold its first violin in Germany in 1887, has grown into a manufacturer producing violins as well as orchestral string instruments such as violas, cellos, and double basses. As the music scene has changed, electric guitars and basses have been added to their lineup, and their instruments are used by world-renowned artists and continue to be loved around the world.

This latest arrival is a model '500/1' bass, widely known as the "violin bass," believed to have been shipped in the late 1960s. As you can see, this is a rare specification with enhanced controls.

【body】
The overall weight is approximately 2.35 kg. The body is made of spruce top and maple sides and back, with a hollow archtop body approximately 11 inches wide and 2 inches thick. The attractive sunburst color is well-suited to the violin shape. There is some weather checking, scratches, and tarnishing on the metal parts, mainly on the body top, but it is in relatively good condition while still retaining the charm of a vintage instrument.

【neck】
The neck is made of two-piece maple. The fingerboard is made of rosewood. The headstock logo is a gold decal type. There are some wood joints, weather checking on the back of the headstock, and some dents with minor paint chipping, but like the body, it is in relatively good condition. The tuners are well-functioning with little variation in winding. The frets appear to have been replaced once, but have been leveled and adjusted by our shop, maintaining excellent neck condition and playability. The nut width is 40.2mm. It features a relatively thick and solid neck shape.

[Electrical System]
The controls are unique, consisting of a 3-way pickup selector, a sound mix ON/OFF switch, a sound mix control, two frequency mix ON/OFF switches, a master tone control, and a master volume control. The SOUND MIX function is a subtle filter effect. The guitar is equipped with two "Bar Blade" pickups, also known as sharkfin pickups, which have been standard equipment since 1967.

The instrument itself has excellent natural resonance, allowing you to fully enjoy that unique woody sound when plugged in, and its rare specification that also lets you enjoy funky filter tones is a major attraction.

*Currently, the setup uses Pyramid strings.

Included case: A slightly worn black hard case.

Weight: 2.35kg
Body Material: Spruce／Maple 2 piece hollow, 11inch
Neck Material/Scale Length: Maple 2 piece/760mm (30inch)
Fingerboard/Fret Marker/Radius: Rosewood w/single binding/Pearloid dot/241.3mm (9inch)
Nut Width/Fret Size: 40.2mm/2.2mm (replaced), 22 Fret
Neck Thickness (1F/7F/12F): 22.3mm/24.2mm/25.2mm
Pickup (Neck/Bridge): Hofner 513B Bar Blade Bass pickup, 6.0kΩ/6.2kΩ

Heartman Vintage Guitars is a specialty store for vintage guitars and basses operated by Ikebe Musical Instruments.

-----------------------------------

[Consignment Sales Service (Items on Hold)]
A left-handed Fender USA 1975 Precision Bass!

With an ash body and maple fingerboard, it boasts a unique attack and richness in the low and high frequencies, and the moderately fat bass is pleasantly impressive.

The date on the neck/pocket is worn away, but the potentiometers are dated 1975 for volume and 1974 for tone, and the pickup is a 1975 gray bobbin (10.6kΩ), so we believe it's a 1975 model. *The DC resistance of the pickup is measured through the control section. The electrical wiring has been repaired, the pickup lead wires have been repaired, and the hardened sponge in the pickup has been replaced.
The guitar comes complete with a pickup fence and bridge cover. The bridge cover has a mute sponge attached, but it has hardened and needs to be replaced before use.

There are some minor scratches and dents, but it is in relatively good condition, with the truss rod functioning well and the neck in good condition. There are signatures written on both the front and back of the headstock.
The frets have been leveled in our repair workshop, so the playability is in excellent condition. Flatwound strings are installed, allowing you to enjoy a boomy sound.

This rare left-handed model boasts a stylish look thanks to its walnut finish and rosewood fingerboard.

Weight ≈ 4.10 kg
Accessories: Fender hard case

The Made in Japan Traditional 2025 Collection '60s Jazz Bass® is a special model based on the Made in Japan Traditional Jazz Bass® with a look exclusive to 2025. "Stardust Pink" and "Stardust Blue" are characterized by a color that blends "glitter" into the pastel colors that are popular these days, and are finished with Fender's unique burst. item.title: Musicvox Spaceranger Bass - Sunburst — $964.90
item.description:
$964.90 · Excellent · PD's Gear Depot

Bring out your inner Funk Soul Brother!

This is a great shortscale bass from the US based company Musicvox. Their innovative designs and great sounding instruments has reached quite a reputation, not least since the Spaceranger being played in the Austin Power's movie Goldmember. This sunburst model is no longer being made.

But the outrageous design is not only for looks: The long body horn (known as "the goldmember") makes the bass well balanced and also very comfortable playing sitting down, and the big head makes for extra neck resonance.

The tonal capacities are very good, as heard in the the demo video. (Note that this bass has two factory mounted string trees, while the demo bass - for some reason - has only one.)

The quite flat and broad neck and the low action makes extra comfortable for finger play and slapping, as well as plectrum play.

This bass is in excellent condition with almost no fret wear and just a few small marks on the body, as seen in the pictures. Please enquire if more pictures are wanted, or for any other questions about the bass.

Scale: 30"

Frets: 20

Neck: Maple with rosewood

Body: Mahogany

Hardware: Kluson style tuners and  Tune-o-Matic bridge, stop tailpiece.

Pickups: Two Musicvox bass humbuckers

Electronics: 3-way pickup selector and controls for volume and tone.

Weight: 3.6 kg

item.title: Black Smoker TRAD MASTER BETA-J5 Ash #26A38 4.13kg［5-string］［Made in Japan］［TM BETA J5］［YK012］ 2026 - -3Tone Sunburst / Midium Aged Lacquer — $2,911.95
item.description:
$2,911.95 · Brand New · G-CLUB Produced by Kurosawa Music

---------------------------------

To customers in Russian Federation,


Due to COVID-19 outbreak, shipping service temporarily terminated from Japan to Russia.

For the above reason, we would like you to do NOT proceed order/transaction.

Welcome to Kurosawa Yokohama! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!


Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!


----------------------------------

The Black Smoker Tradmaster, immensely popular for its high-quality sound and playability, is a testament to the finest craftsmanship. It boasts carefully selected materials, meticulous and handcrafted aged lacquer finish, and solid, precise construction. The 5-string model, in particular, is highly sought after and in high demand, resulting in limited production and a long-standing "backorder" status. But finally, a highly anticipated, high-quality 5-string model has arrived!


Furthermore, this particular model features a rare ash body with block inlays, neck binding, and a 70s-inspired design, boasting excellent wood grain and a superb appearance!


Black Smoker by Black Cloud is a highly acclaimed Japanese brand loved by numerous artists for its overwhelming quality and cost-performance.


Made by a small, elite team of two highly skilled craftsmen—Mr. Kuroiwa, one of Japan's leading guitar builders, and Mr. Yanagisawa, whom he trusts—they produce high-quality instruments despite their limited monthly production.


The Tradmaster series features an all-lacquer finish, resulting in a vintage-inspired sound and look. This guitar features a beautifully aged, medium-aged finish, with weather checks and parts that have been naturally worn for a natural feel. Combined with the stunning grain of the ash body, it boasts a truly luxurious and impressive appearance.


In terms of sound, the all-lacquer finish, original pickups, and high-quality ash body combine to produce a solid, punchy low end and bright, clear highs. However, it avoids being overly skewed towards a scooped midrange, offering a wide tonal range and allowing for endless tonal shaping with volume and tone controls. It's a fun and enjoyable instrument.


The sound is clear, with a fast attack, tight, vivid, and refreshing tone.


The neck shape is a renowned C-shape, ensuring comfortable playability even with a 5-string. Furthermore, the series/parallel switching allows for a wide range of sound variations, and despite being passive, it offers an active approach to playing style and musical genre, making it an all-around instrument.


I believe this is a lively, fun, and comfortable instrument to play, and I highly recommend it.


Since this model has a very limited number of units in stock, don't miss this opportunity!



SPEC

item.title: Fujigen/Fgn NJB100RALV-BK- #C250267 4.57kg ［5-string］［Made in Japan］［Neo Classic Series］［YK012］ 2025 - Black Gross — $991.42

Welcome to Kurosawa Yokohama! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!


Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!


----------------------------------

This 5-string bass boasts a stylish and classic design, featuring an alder body, rosewood fingerboard, and a beautiful black finish.


While based on the traditional JB style, this electric bass incorporates new elements to meet modern needs.


The high-quality construction is evident, and the smooth fret edge treatment, honed through years of experience, supports effortless fingering. Many users praise the neck grip, noting its comfortable, neither too thick nor too thin.


Luminlay (phosphorescent) is used for the side position markers, improving visibility.


The neck joint plate, a crucial element of this detachable (bolt-on) bass, is made of stainless steel. Emphasis is placed on quick attack, and even the fixing screws are made of stainless steel.


The pickups are FGN originals.


The controls are the familiar 2 Volume and 1 Tone configuration. The tone pot is a push-pull switch; pulling it up switches the connection method between the front and rear pickups from the usual parallel to series. This allows you to switch to a more powerful sound, while maintaining the clean, well-behaved tone you get with both pickups at full volume.


A beautifully designed, classic bass. Please consider it.


Weight: 4.57kg

Accessories: Original gig bag


Please inquire for details.

Body Alder

Neck　Maple U-Shape

Fingerboard　Rosewood

item.title: Fujigen/Fgn NJB200RAL-BG- #B260029 4.39kg ［Made in Japan］［Neo Classic Series］［YK012］ 2026 - British Green -Top Lacquer Gloss — $1,094.35
item.description:
$1,094.35 · Brand New · G-CLUB Produced by Kurosawa Music

---------------------------------

To customers in Russian Federation,


Due to COVID-19 outbreak, shipping service temporarily terminated from Japan to Russia.

For the above reason, we would like you to do NOT proceed order/transaction.


-------------------------------------------


Welcome to Kurosawa Yokohama! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!


Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!


----------------------------------

This bass features an alder body, rosewood fingerboard, and a sophisticated British green finish. As a higher-end 200-grade model, it boasts Seymour Duncan pickups and a superior topcoat lacquer finish.


The craftsmanship is exceptional, and the smooth fret edge treatment, honed over many years of experience, is evident. The neck grip is also highly praised by many users for its comfortable, neither too thick nor too thin profile.


Luminlay (phosphorescent) side position markers enhance visibility.


The neck joint plate, a crucial element of this detachable (bolt-on) bass, is made of stainless steel. Emphasis is placed on quick response, and even the fixing screws are stainless steel.


The bridge is the highly popular GOTOH J510SJ-4, known for its rich, resonant string vibrations transmitted to the body.


The pickups are a combination of Seymour Duncan SJB-1n and SJB-1b. This guitar aims for a traditional JB sound.

The controls are the familiar 2 Volume and 1 Tone configuration. The Tone pot is a push-pull switch; pulling it up switches the connection method between the front and rear pickups from the usual parallel to series. This allows you to switch to a more powerful sound while maintaining the clean, well-behaved tone you get with both volume knobs at full.


Weight: 4.39kg

Accessories: Original gig bag


Please inquire for details.


Construction Bolt-on Neck

Body Alder

Neck　Maple U-Shape

Fingerboard Rosewood

Scale　34" (864mm)

Frets　20F Medium Jumbo C.F.S.

Tuners GOTOH® GB2

Bridge GOTOH® J510SJ-4 (String Pitch = 19mm)

Hardware Color　Chrome & Nickel

Pickup (Neck) Seymour Duncan® Vintage SJB-1n

Pickup (Bridge) Seymour Duncan® Vintage SJB-1b

Controls　2Volume, 1Tone w/ Push-Pull SW (Series/Parallel Select SW)

Body Finish　Top Lacquer Gloss

item.description:
$920.99 · Brand New · G-CLUB Produced by Kurosawa Music

---------------------------------

To customers in Russian Federation,

Welcome to Kurosawa Yokohama! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!


Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!


----------------------------------

The overwhelmingly popular new color, the beautiful Candy Apple Green!


This is a modified version with a black pickguard!


*The photo was taken with a protective film applied to the pickguard.


The "NJB" from the Neo-Classic series, featuring the classic combination of an alder body and rosewood fingerboard, has been upgraded and is now in stock!


While based on the traditional JB style, this electric bass incorporates new elements to meet modern needs.


In addition to its excellent craftsmanship, it utilizes techniques cultivated over many years of experience, such as fret edge treatment that supports smooth fingering. Many users have also praised the neck grip, saying it's "neither too thick nor too thin and easy to hold."


Newly changed features include the truss rod adjustment position being moved to the neck joint, and the use of Luminlay (phosphorescent) for the side position markers for improved visibility.


The neck joint plate, the heart of this detachable (bolt-on) bass, is made of stainless steel. Prioritizing excellent sound attack, even the mounting screws are made of stainless steel, demonstrating meticulous attention to detail.


The pickups are FGN originals.


The controls are the familiar 2 Volume and 1 Tone configuration. The Tone pot is a push-pull switch; pulling it up switches the connection method between the front and rear pickups from the usual parallel to series. This allows you to switch to a powerful sound that's a step forward, while maintaining the clean, well-behaved tone when both volumes are at full.


A quintessential, classic bass. Please consider it.

item.title: Fender Standard Jazz Bass 2012 MIM — $658.83
item.description:
$658.83 · Good · Astronauts Guitars

Fender Standard Jazz Bass


Key Features

– 2012 Mexico-made Standard Series Jazz Bass, the precursor to the Player Series line

– The classic alder / maple / rosewood specs deliver Standard Jazz Bass tone that works across any genre

– Gloss-finished headstock and a naturally aged white finish with mint green guard for an elegant and sharp vibe

– It has minor marks and finish yellowing, but overall it remains in relatively good used condition.


This is a straightforward, easy-to-handle bass, making it a great choice both for a beginner and as a reliable backup for a more experienced player.


Specs

– Body: Alder

– Neck: Maple

– Fingerboard: Rosewood

– Scale: Long (34”/864mm)

– Frets: 20

– Weight: 4.2kg

– Year: 2012

– Included: Generic soft case


Playability

– Straight neck with no twisting or warping

– Both directions of the truss rod work properly

– Comfortable, easy-to-manage setup

– A practical player’s bass that works well as a first serious instrument or a reliable backup


Condition

– Parts replaced: None

– Damages: Finish yellowing, dings, scuffs, and small finish chips

– Neck: Straight, with no warp or twist

– Frets: Still have solid life left; the lowest point measures 0.9mm, about 80% left

– Truss rod: Works fine in both directions


Setup

– 12th fret action: 2.0mm 1st string / 2.5mm 4th string


Recommended Player

A beginner looking for a straightforward, easy-to-play Standard Jazz Bass that can support you in any future direction, or for a more experienced player considering a reliable backup Jazz Bass that suits a wide range of genres.

Questions or extra photos? Feel free to contact us. If you’re ready, buy now and make it yours.


Please note

- We are unable to accept price negotiations, as the listed price already reflects our best.

- The shipping fees listed on the item page are reference prices and may differ from the actual shipping cost (including Reverb and PayPal fees.) While the amounts are accurate for many countries, they may vary significantly for others.

- All customs taxes and VAT are the buyers’ responsibility. Please check out the requirements for your country.

- All items are also for sale in our physical workshop in Takadanobaba. There is a chance they might be sold out.

- We sell only at Reverb for customers from outside Japan.

- We accept payment only through Reverb platform.

item.title: Atelier Z babyZ-4J Active -Orange- #252369[3.65kg][Short scale][IKE011] — $954.53
item.description:
$954.53 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

------------------------------------------------------------

[Tokyo Musical Instruments Expo 2025 Limited Edition Model]

Since its establishment in 1989, ATELIER Z has continuously developed instruments through collaborations with top-tier bassists—such as the late Tomohito Aoki—and has created numerous masterpieces by adopting a unique approach that incorporates professional-grade setups and active circuitry.

The reliable sound, crafted by integrating feedback from professional bassists, has garnered support from a wide range of players, from professionals to amateurs.


"ATELIER Z DESIGN" is a new series that achieves excellent cost-performance through overseas production.

This particular instrument is a special model: originally produced as a limited run for the Tokyo Musical Instrument Fair 2025, it has since undergone an "active modification" at ATELIER Z's Tokyo workshop.


It features authentic specifications throughout, utilizing an alder body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard.

It is equipped with "New Baby-4J" pickups, developed specifically for this model.


Despite its charming appearance, the sound delivers power combined with the soft, warm low-end texture characteristic of a short-scale bass.

The orange finish further amplifies its cuteness to the max!

Yet, the sound is strictly professional-grade. While offering the benefits of a short-scale instrument, it also delivers clear articulation and a crisp, punchy response when switched to active mode.


Thanks to the unique pitch characteristics of the short scale and powerful, original Japanese-made pickups that highlight the presence of each string, this model achieves the signature ATELIER Z "Magnitude Sound"—reminiscent of the brand's famous M#245 and M#265 models.


With a US-made Bartolini XTCT preamp at its heart, it is truly a "little giant" overflowing with power!


Whether as a primary instrument, a casual bass for home practice, or a choice for guitarists and beginners alike, this is a bass we hope a wide variety of players will pick up and enjoy!


We look forward to your inquiries.
-----------------------------------

【SPEC】


Body: Alder

Neck: Maple/Rosewood (Carbon Support Bar)

Scale: 30"

Frets: 19

Tuners: Elephant Ear style

Bridge: 2-saddle bass bridge (18P)

Pickups: ATELIER Z New babyZ-4J x 2

Preamp: Bartolini XTCT

Controls: Front Vol / Rear Vol, Master Tone / Switch (Active ON/OFF), Treble / Bass


Weight: Approx. 3.65 kg

Included: Dedicated BabyZ gig bag


------------------------------------

item.title: Warwick Pro Series Corvette $$ 4st Limited Edition 2023 — $4,200
item.description:
$4,200 · Excellent · Gemini13

Purchased in November 2025 brand new and seldom played. Still in Like-New condition with a pro setup and no cosmetic defects.

limited edition 2023 model, only 100 pcs. worldwide (4- and 5-string combined)

  • right-hand version
  • 4-string electric bass
  • 34" (864 mm) long scale
  • flat body shape
  • 3/8" selected Marbled Ebony top (Masterreserve)
  • Roasted Swamp Ash body
  • 5-piece Roasted Flamed Maple neck with Ekanga stripes (C shape)
  • Tigerstripe Ebony fretboard
  • 24 brass jumbo frets, extra high, extra hard (width: 0.11" (2.9 mm) / height: 0.05" (1.3 mm))
  • Invisible Fret Technology (IFT)
  • F clef (bass clef) inlay at 12th fret
  • 20" fretboard radius
  • Just-A-Nut III (brass) nut
  • 1.5" (38.5 mm) nut width
  • passive MEC MM-Style quad coil humbucker pickup with brushed metal cover (neck)
  • passive MEC MM-Style quad coil humbucker pickup with brushed metal cover (bridge)
  • active MEC 3-way electronics
  • controls for Volume (P/P) / Balance / Mid / Treble and Bass (stacked)
  • push/pull function for preamp bypass
  • 2x 3-way toggle switch for humbucker pickup configuration (series / parallel / single coils
  • Warwick 2-piece 3D bridge and tailpiece (brass)
  • Natural Oil finish
  • black hardware
  • incl. Warwick Masterbuilt Genuine Leather User Kit - Black (RB PROD USER KIT WBK)
  • incl. individual Certificate of Authenticity
  • made in Germany
